sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 4-year-old mother, presents with a complaint of a painful right knee that has been bothering her since a skiing accident last weekend.
She reports that her knee popped and she was unable to bear weight.
She describes the pain as feeling like her knee is going to buckle and is worse when she tries to straighten it.
She denies numbness or tingling in her toes and can feel her toes.
She has been taking ibuprofen for pain, which has provided some relief.
She is generally healthy, with no history of high blood pressure, diabetes, or other medical conditions.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: Ibuprofen 600-800mg every 8 hours as needed for pain.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has edema and an effusion over the right knee, which is painful to palpation.
There is decreased flexion and extension of the right knee, and a positive Lachman sign.
The patient has palpable dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulses, with no leg edema in the ankle.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: I suspect that the patient has a ruptured anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) in her right knee.
Problem list: ACL injury; suspected; confirmed.
Plan:
I ordered a knee MRI to confirm the diagnosis.
I instructed the patient to continue taking ibuprofen 600-800mg every 8 hours as needed for pain.
I also recommended that she wear a knee brace to provide additional support.
I will schedule a follow-up appointment to discuss the results of the MRI and plan for further treatment.
Follow-up: I will see the patient again in a few days to discuss the results of the MRI and plan for further treatment.
